question_text: A guitar string is plucked and produces a note with a frequency of 440 Hz. The wavelength of this sound wave in air is 0.78 m. Calculate the speed of sound in air using the wave equation v = f × λ. Show your working.
explanation: Using v = f × λ, we substitute the values: v = 440 Hz × 0.78 m = 343.2 m/s. This is close to the accepted value of the speed of sound in air at room temperature, which validates the calculation.
